takeaways: Senior housing supply-demand gap keeps widening — Q1 2026 added units equal to just 0.4% of inventory while occupancy sits at 89.5% and independent living tops 91%, the best in a decade. Existing operators win.
REITs are buying everything in sight: Welltower closed 41 deals for $3.2B in Q1 plus a C$4.1B Canadian portfolio; Ventas grabbed Revel's 11 communities for $540M — two-thirds of it off-market.
The Fed is on hold at 3.5–3.75% with the 10-year near 4.4% and $1.5T of CRE loans maturing through 2026 — yet Colliers still forecasts a 15–20% jump in sales volume as capital re-enters.

items.headline: Senior living development hopes fade as operators turn inward
items.summary: Q1 2026 deliveries equaled just 0.4% of total inventory as financing scrutiny stretches project timelines, and Senior Housing News reports operators are shelving bigger 2026 development plans. With occupancy at 89.5% and new supply stalled, pricing power shifts decisively to operators with existing communities — a tailwind for Lloyd Jones' operating portfolio.

items.headline: Welltower and Ventas dominate off-market senior living M&A surge
items.summary: Welltower closed 41 transactions totaling $3.2B in Q1 2026 — 37 communities sourced off-market — plus a C$4.1B 38-community Canadian portfolio, while Ventas bought the 11-community Revel portfolio for $540M. SLIB is on pace to exceed 100 transactions this year. REIT capital is setting the market for quality senior housing assets, lifting valuations for sellers.

items.headline: Fed holds at 3.5–3.75%; no cuts expected for rest of 2026
items.summary: J.P. Morgan Research now sees the Fed steady through 2026 with the next move possibly a hike in 2027, while the 10-year Treasury hovers near 4.4%. For CRE borrowers the message is clear: underwrite to today's rates — lenders are keeping spreads wide on transitional assets even as policy stabilizes.

items.headline: $1.5T CRE maturity wall meets abundant multifamily debt
items.summary: Deloitte's 2026 outlook flags roughly $1.5 trillion in CRE loans maturing through year-end, but multifamily debt capital is plentiful — GSE lending caps rose 20.5%. Refinancing pressure on weaker assets will create acquisition opportunities for well-capitalized buyers in multifamily and senior housing.

items.headline: Property managers using AI broadly expect 31% portfolio growth
items.summary: Propmodo reports the big proptech platforms are splitting on AI agent strategy: CoStar's Homes AI keeps data in a closed ecosystem while AppFolio ties AI to operations — its users applying AI across core workflows expect 31% portfolio growth in 2026 versus 12% for non-users. The AI adoption gap is becoming a measurable competitive moat for operators.

items.headline: Independent living occupancy tops 91% as 'Woodstock Generation' moves in
items.summary: IL occupancy crossed 91% in Q1 — a decade high in both primary and secondary markets — and operators like Cedarhurst are adding IL cottages to meet demand from younger, more active boomers. Product mix matters: IL expansion offers lower-acuity, lower-labor-cost growth for senior housing operators.

items.headline: Energy costs up 17.9% squeeze senior living margins
items.summary: McKnight's reports fuel, energy and insurance costs climbing sharply — energy up 17.9% year over year on oil supply disruption — as the industry enters 2026 'at a critical juncture.' Operators should be locking utility contracts and pushing rate growth to protect NOI.

items.headline: Colliers forecasts 15–20% jump in CRE sales volume this year
items.summary: Institutional and cross-border capital is re-entering US CRE, with confidence building as leasing fundamentals stabilize. For owners weighing dispositions, deeper buyer pools and compressing bid-ask spreads make 2026 a better exit window than the past two years.

items.headline: Commercial mortgage rates range 5.39–8.75% as of June 4
items.summary: Conventional commercial mortgage rates sit between 5.39% and 8.75% depending on property type and credit, with the full commercial loan range spanning 5.06–12.75%. Multifamily and senior housing continue to price at the favorable end thanks to agency liquidity.

items.headline: Agentic AI moves from pilots to procurement, pricing and operations
items.summary: ICSC reports retailers and real estate operators are deploying AI agents for procurement, pricing, inventory and event programming — autonomous execution, not just content generation. Early adopters are banking operational cost advantages; the window to be 'early' is closing.

items.headline: Legora buys Cadastral, its fourth AI acquisition of 2026
items.summary: Legora acquired Cadastral, an AI agent platform for CRE legal intelligence used by major firms — the latest sign of rapid consolidation in AI-native CRE tooling. Expect lease abstraction, diligence and legal review to get dramatically cheaper and faster.

items.headline: Mastercard reshuffles C-suite: Ling Hai named CFO
items.summary: Mastercard named Ling Hai CFO and moved Sachin Mehra into a new chief business officer role unifying country operations, partnerships and go-to-market under one structure. A case study in reorganizing leadership around execution speed rather than legacy reporting lines.

items.headline: 73% of business leaders expect revenue growth in 2026
items.summary: J.P. Morgan's Business Leaders Outlook finds 73% anticipate higher revenue and 64% higher profits this year, with over half planning new products and 41% doubling down on their most profitable offerings. Focus and profitability — not expansion at any cost — define the 2026 playbook.

items.headline: 'Emotional fitness' emerges as 2026's defining wellness category
items.summary: The Global Wellness Summit's 2026 trends name emotional fitness — cognitive performance plus emotional resilience — as a new health category, with employers building complete well-being ecosystems rather than one-off perks. Relevant for both workplace culture and senior living programming.

items.headline: Culture becomes the differentiator as AI levels the tech playing field
items.summary: DDI's 2026 leadership trends report puts leadership development back at the top of HR priorities — organizations with strong leadership are twice as likely to excel at innovation. As AI tools become commoditized, how teams collaborate, decide and handle pressure is what separates performers.

items.headline: Springs Living breaks ground in Washington; Momentum underway in California
items.summary: The Springs Living started construction in Washington and Momentum Senior Living is underway in California, while Cedarhurst adds 24 IL cottages in Wichita opening mid-2027. Scattered starts notwithstanding, the national pipeline remains historically thin.

items.headline: 'Movement snacks' replace the hour-long workout for desk workers
items.summary: Short bursts of movement and mobility scattered through the day are 2026's answer to sedentary work — supporting joint health, metabolism and focus. An easy, zero-cost wellness practice for office teams and resident programming alike.

items.headline: Longevity goes mainstream as a full lifestyle, not a supplement stack
items.summary: 2026 longevity practice blends metabolic optimization, functional nutrition, strength training, sleep and community connection — with AI wellness coaches personalizing the mix. Senior living operators are well positioned to package this as a differentiated resident experience.
